# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Istio IngressGateway configuration that routes HTTP traffic to services. Configure Gateway for port 80 without TLS for simplicity. Use HTTP for all ingress traffic since we have a load balancer handling TLS termination upstream. This is for our Istio service mesh ingress.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: Gateway
metadata:
  name: http-gateway
spec:
  selector:
    istio: ingress-gateway   # Selects the Istio Ingress gateway for this service mesh and listens on port 80 (port specified by `http_port` below). You can also select it with other labels.
     httpPort: "80"          // This specifies which Port to use as ingress; must match if Service traffic is used too or not at all, for example using '42' in service-to-service and server port definitions here might be different than the actual ports defined elsewhere (like VirtualService/Envoy filter configs)
  servers:                     # Define what services to expose. If none are specified then it defaults to a single IP address of ingress gateway control plane being exposed via Istio Ingress Gateway's port with HTTP protocol in use ie `localhost` or the actual host and public ip for your load balancer
  - port:                        # Ports which this service should serve on. You may specify multiple ports per server entry if you need to match against a header field (or set of fields). If not specified, it defaults towards all available non-terminated TCP & UDP ports in the pod definition and HTTP protocols listed above
    number: 80                     # Ports can be numbers between `1` -> `65535` inclusive. Zero means to use default value from Istio Ingress Gateway settings, if specified different then this port is used for ingress (HTTP/HTTPS) traffic
    name: "http"                  # Port names can be any string that makes sense in the context of your system or process; not required but recommended. If omitted a unique default value will be generated by Istio using 'default-namespace' and `name` combined with one other field (like port number) as final fallback
    hostname: "*"                  # Hosts are used to specify which IP addresses should serve the traffic for your service in addition, or left empty if you want services on all available non-terminated TCP & UDP ports. If omitted `*` will be assumed and it serves default port 80 (HTTP/HTTPS)
